item.text: The package explanations confirm the main direct fixes: `axios`, `express`, `jsonwebtoken`, and `lodash` all have fixed releases; `request` itself does not. For `request`’s vulnerable children, I’m going to prefer targeted `overrides` and then verify the app, since replacing `request` would be a behavior change outside the requested scope.

The app does exercise `request` in tests, so the override set needs to be tested for runtime compatibility, not just lockfile resolution. I’m about to update the manifest and lockfile via npm, then I’ll run `make test` and rescan.

item.text: `uuid@11.1.1` closes the advisory but breaks `request`, which imports the old `uuid/v4` subpath. I’m checking whether any compatible `uuid` release fixes that advisory; if not, I’ll remove that override and document that this transitive vulnerability cannot be remediated without replacing `request`.

item.text: I found the core problem: `request@2.88.2` requires the legacy `uuid/v4` path, while the fixed `uuid` line no longer exports it. Since the user requirement prioritizes keeping the app building and tests passing, I’m removing only that incompatible override and leaving the rest of the remediation in place.

Found transitive `uuid@3.4.0` via `request` with GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq. Did not keep a version override: the reported fixed line starts at `uuid@11.1.1`, but `request@2.88.2` imports the legacy `uuid/v4` subpath that is not exported by `uuid@11.1.1`, causing the build to fail. No compatible fixed `uuid` version exists for `request@2.88.2`; fixing this would require replacing or patching `request`, which is outside version-only remediation.
